origin.name: Tunisia
origin.key_document: Extrait d'acte de deces (death certificate, requires certified English translation)
origin.source: FCDO Tunisia travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Tunis guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation is not permitted under Tunisian law. Full body repatriation is the only option.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Extrait d'acte de deces (certified English translation required)
special_rules: All French or Arabic-language documentation requires certified English translation for UK acceptance.
Police clearance is required for all violent or suspicious deaths.
An export permit from Tunisian authorities is required for all repatriations.
Embalming must be carried out by a licensed funeral director before air transport.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Tunisian documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Tunis can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Tunisia to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Tunisia. Repatriation to the UK takes 2-3 weeks. Civil registry, French and Arabic translation, and export permits all handled. Contact us 24/7.
